suggestions.topic: Glamsterdam DevNet 0 status and mid-April launch readiness
suggestions.rationale: DevNet 0 was targeted for mid-April (before Interop) as of ACDC #176. With April 6 approaching the deadline, teams need to confirm launch readiness or adjust timeline.

suggestions.topic: ePBS critical issues resolution: PTC fix, 4788 gaps, reorg handling
suggestions.rationale: ACDC #176 identified multiple blocking issues for ePBS DevNet 1, including the PTC fix forcing a hard fork, 4788 payload gaps requiring stakeholder feedback, and engine API reorg semantics. These need status updates.

suggestions.topic: SSZ Engine API implementation decision following PR 764 review
suggestions.rationale: ACDC #176 surfaced significant debate on whether to ship SSZ transport immediately vs. redesign entire Engine API. This was deferred to ACDE for cross-team discussion and needs CL perspective.

suggestions.topic: Cell-level deltas deployment timeline and Glamsterdam inclusion
suggestions.rationale: ACDC #174 reported Blob DevNet Zero running stable with multiple implementations in progress. As a backwards-compatible optimization that can deploy pre-Amsterdam, teams should confirm deployment plans.

suggestions.topic: FOCIL (Hegotá headliner) implementation progress across clients
suggestions.rationale: FOCIL was selected as Hegotá headliner in ACDC #175. ACDC #176 mentioned non-headliner proposal window opens after EL headliner decision. Teams should provide implementation status updates.

suggestions.topic: Hegotá non-headliner proposals following EL decision
suggestions.rationale: ACDC #176 noted that non-headliner proposal window opens after EL headliner decision next week (late March). If EL headliner was decided, CL teams should discuss corresponding proposals like EIP-8148 (custom sweep threshold).

suggestions.topic: Partial slot proofs implementation status (Teku, Lodestar, Nimbus)
suggestions.rationale: ACDC #173 established that ePBS cannot ship without partial slot proofs in all clients. ACDC #174 reported Lighthouse/Prism implementations in DevNet, but Teku/Lodestar/Nimbus status remains unclear.
